applied_optimizations.name: Triton min reduction kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Replaced PyTorch torch.min with a Triton kernel for min reduction along channel dimension. The kernel uses BLOCK_SIZE=8192 (next power of 2) aligned to 64 for AMD wavefronts.

attempted_but_failed.name: Fused GroupNorm + Min Triton kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: GroupNorm kernel produced incorrect results - mean/variance computation in loops did not match PyTorch behavior
attempted_but_failed.error: Max diff of 0.8+ between Triton and PyTorch GroupNorm outputs

notes: GEMM dominates at 85.6% - already using optimized rocBLAS/hipBLAS kernel (Cijk_Alik_Bljk_...)
GroupNorm is second at 10.3% - fused kernel attempt failed due to incorrect mean/var computation
Min reduction is only 1.2% of runtime - Triton optimization gives minimal benefit
AMD ROCm Triton constraints: BLOCK_SIZE should be multiple of 64 for wavefront alignment
Final speedup is limited by GEMM dominance - post-GEMM operations are only ~14% of total time

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all crashed with zero output. The agent needs extremely minimal, concrete instructions. torch.compile has never been attempted and is the safest optimization path for this GEMM-boun

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 crashed with zero output — 4th consecutive crash. With ~40 min left, one more attempt with extremely minimal, concrete instructions. The agent needs a near-copy-paste solution to avoid crashin

global_notes: Problem 75 is GEMM-bound (85%+). Triton kernels can only optimize the remaining 15%.
The Triton min kernel is correct and provides small speedup.
Further optimization would require fusing GroupNorm, but this proved difficult to implement correctly.
